title: Set up an Expo React Native project from scratch
summary: Complete Expo setup flow ‚Äî create-expo-app, Expo Router, native modules, EAS Build, env vars, OTA updates, and common pitfalls.
declaration: I will set up a new Expo React Native project using the official Expo toolchain. If you prefer bare React Native CLI or a different template, say so before I begin.
instructions:
## Set Up Expo React Native Project

### 1. Create the project
- Use `npx create-expo-app@latest <project-name>` to scaffold.
- If user wants a specific template: `npx create-expo-app@latest <name> --template tabs` (or `blank`, `blank-typescript`).
- Default template uses Expo Router with file-based routing ‚Äî confirm this is desired.
- `cd` into the project directory after creation.

### 2. Verify initial setup
- Run `npx expo start` to confirm the dev server launches.
- Confirm Expo Go connects on a physical device or simulator.
- If using a simulator: iOS requires Xcode + Simulator installed. Android requires Android Studio + an AVD configured.

### 3. Expo Router setup (if not using tabs template)
- Install: `npx expo install expo-router expo-linking expo-constants expo-status-bar`.
- Create `app/` directory at project root for file-based routes.
- Add entry point in `package.json`: `"main": "expo-router/entry"`.
- Create `app/_layout.tsx` as the root layout.
- Create `app/index.tsx` as the home screen.
- Add `scheme` to `app.json` for deep linking.

### 4. Native modules (Expo Modules)
- For camera, notifications, location, etc., use `npx expo install <package>`.
- Always prefer `expo install` over `npm install` ‚Äî it pins SDK-compatible versions.
- Common modules: `expo-camera`, `expo-notifications`, `expo-location`, `expo-image-picker`, `expo-secure-store`, `expo-haptics`.
- If a native module requires a config plugin, add it to `app.json` under `"plugins"`.
- After adding native modules that need config plugins, run `npx expo prebuild` to regenerate native projects.

### 5. Environment variables
- Use `EXPO_PUBLIC_` prefix for client-side env vars (e.g., `EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL`).
- Access via `process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL` ‚Äî works in Expo SDK 49+.
- For secrets (API keys that must not leak to the client), use EAS Secrets: `eas secret:create --name MY_SECRET --value xxx`.
- Never commit `.env` files. Add `.env*` to `.gitignore`.

### 6. EAS Build setup
- Install EAS CLI: `npm install -g eas-cli`.
- Login: `eas login`.
- Initialize: `eas build:configure` ‚Äî generates `eas.json`.
- Build profiles in `eas.json`:
  - `development` ‚Äî internal distribution, includes dev client.
  - `preview` ‚Äî internal distribution, production-like but not store-ready.
  - `production` ‚Äî store-ready build.
- Development build: `eas build --profile development --platform ios` (or `android`).
- Production build: `eas build --profile production --platform all`.
- For local builds (no EAS cloud): `eas build --local`.

### 7. OTA Updates (EAS Update)
- Install: `npx expo install expo-updates`.
- Configure in `app.json`: set `updates.url` and `runtimeVersion`.
- Run `eas update:configure` to set up.
- Publish an update: `eas update --branch production --message "description"`.
- OTA updates only work for JS/asset changes ‚Äî native module changes require a new build.
- Use `runtimeVersion` policy `"appVersion"` or `"fingerprint"` to control compatibility.

### 8. Project structure recommendation
```
app/               # Expo Router screens
  _layout.tsx      # Root layout
  index.tsx        # Home
  (tabs)/          # Tab group
components/        # Reusable components
lib/               # Utilities, API clients
constants/         # Colors, config
assets/            # Images, fonts
```

### Common errors & fixes
- **Metro cache stale / weird errors after install** ‚Üí `npx expo start --clear` or `rm -rf node_modules/.cache`.
- **iOS Simulator not found** ‚Üí Open Xcode > Settings > Platforms > install iOS simulator runtime.
- **Android SDK not found** ‚Üí Set `ANDROID_HOME` env var. Run `sdkmanager` to install platform tools.
- **'Unable to resolve module'** ‚Üí Clear Metro cache. If persists, delete `node_modules` and reinstall.
- **'Invariant Violation: Native module cannot be null'** ‚Üí You added a native module but didn't rebuild. Run `npx expo prebuild --clean` then `npx expo run:ios` or `run:android`.
- **EAS build fails with 'credentials'** ‚Üí Run `eas credentials` to configure signing. iOS needs an Apple Developer account.
- **OTA update not appearing** ‚Üí Check `runtimeVersion` matches between build and update. Mismatched versions silently skip updates.
- **Expo Go can't run native modules** ‚Üí Expo Go only supports modules in the Expo SDK. For custom native code, use a development build (`eas build --profile development`).

### What NOT to do
- Do NOT use `npm install` for Expo SDK packages ‚Äî always use `npx expo install` to get compatible versions.
- Do NOT eject (`expo eject` is deprecated). Use `npx expo prebuild` for native customization.
- Do NOT modify `ios/` or `android/` directories directly if using managed workflow ‚Äî changes get overwritten by prebuild.
- Do NOT put secrets in `EXPO_PUBLIC_` vars ‚Äî they are embedded in the JS bundle and visible to anyone.
- Do NOT skip the development build step and rely solely on Expo Go for apps with native modules.
- Do NOT run `eas build --profile production` without testing on a preview/development build first.
- Do NOT ignore `runtimeVersion` mismatches ‚Äî they cause OTA updates to silently fail.
